title: Imperial Sourcebook (Second Edition)
content:
{{Top|rwm|ref|reb|italics=1}}
{{Youmay|the second edition of the book|the [[Imperial Sourcebook|first edition]]}}
{{ReferenceBook
|image=[[File:Imperialsourcebook-2ndedition.jpg]]
|title=''Imperial Sourcebook''
|author=[[Greg Gorden]]
|cover artist=

|release date=[[July]] [[1994]]<ref name="Date">{{JournalCite|2|New Horizons}}</ref>

The '''''Imperial Sourcebook''''', by [[Greg Gorden]], was a [[West End Games]] supplement for their version of the [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game|Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]. It discussed the organization of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in the aftermath of the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. This, the second edition, was released in [[June]] [[1994]], following the release of the sourcebook's [[Imperial Sourcebook|first edition]] in [[October]] [[1989]]. It was designed to be an in-universe report on the Empire compiled for [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] by [[Arhul Hextrophon/Legends|Arhul Hextrophon]].

The ''Imperial Sourcebook'' "is set in that turbulent [[Time/Legends|time]] that begins with the destruction of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]]" and "looks at the might of the Empire after [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars IV: A New Hope'']]."

{{Book-stub}}

==Opening crawl==
{{OpeningCrawl
|name=
|text=It is a dark time for the Rebellion. Although the original Death Star has been destroyed, Imperial forces have instituted a reign of terror throughout the galaxy.

The Galactic Empire has many weapons in its arsenal and every one of them has been turned upon the Rebels: tyranny, oppression, vast fleets, overwhelming armies and fear, the prime motivator in the New Order.

The upstart Rebel Alliance has dared win a significant battle against the Empire at Yavin, but the war has only begun. Outnumbered and outgunned, the Rebellion nevertheless burns across a thousand-thousand worlds, flaming into a fire of hope.

But the Emperor has ordered this fire extinguished, setting the massive war machine he has constructed into motion ...}}

==Contents==
===Introduction===
===Prologue===
===Chapter One: A Primer on Imperial Power===
*''[[By His Own Hand]]''
*[[Tarkin Doctrine/Legends|''Imperial Communique #001044.92v'']]

===Chapter Two: [[Commission for the Preservation of the New Order/Legends|COMPNOR]]===
===Chapter Three: [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]]===
*''[[How to Read an Imperial Intelligence Scandoc]]''

===Chapter Four: [[Imperial Military/Legends|The Military]]===
*''[[Imperial Spin]]''
*''[[Admiral Jerjerrod's Testimony]]''

===Chapter Five: [[Capital ship/Legends|Capital Ships]]===
===Chapter Six: Custom Ordnance===
===Chapter Seven: Land Vehicles===
===Chapter Eight: [[Sector/Legends|Sector]] Group Organization===
*''[[Picutorion Viewed From the Top]]''
*''[[A Salvager's Run]]''
*''[[The Full Might of the Empire]]''

===Chapter Nine: Combat Artillery===
===Chapter Ten: Infantry Support Weapons===
===Chapter Eleven: Special Military Forces===
*''[[Death of a Rebel Base]]''
*''[[The Assault on Protazk]]''

===Chapter Twelve: Recruitment, Training, and Indoctrination===

==Development==
The second edition of ''Imperial Sourcebook'' was first published in [[June]] [[1994]]. A second printing was released in [[January]] [[1995]].
